npm.release_policy: exact package in the bootstrap-owned frozen lock

config_schema.selected_schema_reason: MiMoCode is an OpenCode fork; its project config is OpenCode-config compatible and the OpenCode schema URL resolves. The earlier https://mimo.xiaomi.com//config.json value had a doubled slash and returned HTTP 404. Upstream MiMo-Code also selects the OpenCode schema.
config_schema.schema_extension_note: MiMoCode-native keys (for example checkpoint and maxMode) extend the OpenCode schema and may surface as editor schema warnings; runtime config correctness is enforced by scripts/validate_mimocode_config.py, not by the remote schema.
config_schema.jsonc_support_note: MiMoCode reads `.mimocode/mimocode.jsonc` (preferred) or `.mimocode/mimocode.json`; the runtime parses JSONC via jsonc-parser with comments and trailing commas allowed, so the committed `.jsonc` source is runtime-native and needs no `.json` projection.
config_schema.runtime_schema_validation_method: scripts/validate_mimocode_config.py validates the committed config against the resolvable OpenCode schema (JSON Schema 2020-12, https://opencode.ai/config.json) plus the required MiMoCode-native key set; the permission object uses additionalProperties so native keys pass schema validation.
config_schema.runtime_introspection_note: MiMoCode exposes no `mimo config validate` or `doctor` subcommand; `mimo debug config` dumps the resolved merged config and requires the installed binary, so it is an optional installed-runtime check, not a static release gate.

forbidden_as_stable: npm metadata without GitHub release cross-check
